core_mechanism: Bogoliubov collective-excitation spectrum of a macroscopically occupied, weakly-interacting boson condensate: interparticle coupling turns the free-particle quadratic dispersion into a linear (sound-like) spectrum at long wavelength, yielding a Landau critical-velocity threshold for dissipationless flow
brief_reason: The abstract builds its whole argument on a Bose-Einstein condensate of interacting bosons whose low-lying excitations obey a Bogoliubov dispersion relation giving long-wavelength sound waves and a Landau critical-velocity criterion for superfluidity.
